title: astragalus cibarius
intro: Astragalus cibarius, also known as the mountain milkvetch, is a perennial legume native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and edible roots. The medicinal properties of Astragalus cibarius lie in its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to boost the immune system and fight off infections. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and enhance liver function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Astragalus cibarius is typically prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used as a dietary supplement in capsule or powder form.
